question: Will CPI rise more than 1.1% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been released on April 10, 2026. Multiple sources confirm the monthly CPI increase was between 0.8% and 0.9%, with most reliable sources citing 0.9%. Since the market asks if CPI will rise MORE than 1.1% in March 2026, and the actual increase was approximately 0.9%, the market should resolve to NO. However, given the current YES price of 3%, there appears to be significant market mispricing.
key_factors: March 2026 CPI data already released showing ~0.9% monthly increase
Multiple credible sources confirm increase was below 1.1% threshold
Energy price shocks from Iran conflict drove the increase but insufficient to breach 1.1%
Market resolution should be NO based on official BLS data
risks: Potential data revision by BLS (though unlikely to change by 0.2+ percentage points)
Misinterpretation of resolution criteria or data source
Market may be pricing in small probability of measurement error
